top_findings.title: Acceptance suite never validates the app under scoring (test_coverage=0.14, app.py ~0% covered)
top_findings.evidence: test_app.py:13 and conftest.py:17 issue HTTP requests to a live server, but no fixture/harness starts one; scores.json test_coverage=0.14 and .coverage shows app.py essentially unexercised — all 25 tests error on connection when run headless via `pytest --cov`.
top_findings.suggestion: Start the Flask app in-process for tests (Flask test_client or a session-scoped fixture that launches/tears down the server) so the acceptance suite is self-hosting and actually exercises app.py.

top_findings.title: Port mismatch: app.py binds 5000 but tests/conftest/README target 5001
top_findings.evidence: app.py:217 `app.run(..., port=5000, ...)` vs test_app.py:13 and conftest.py:8 `BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5001` and README.md:73 'server starts at 5001'. Following the README, `python app.py` binds 5000 and every test fails to connect.
top_findings.suggestion: Make the port consistent — bind 5000→5001 in app.py (or read from env) so the documented run instructions actually make the tests pass.

top_findings.title: R12: >=3 tests exist but do not effectively run/pass (coverage 0.14)
top_findings.evidence: 25 tests defined in test_app.py, but they require an externally-started server on the wrong port, so none pass under scoring; test_coverage=0.14 barely clears >0 and no behavior is actually verified.
top_findings.suggestion: Make at least the core CRUD/health tests self-hosting so they pass without manual setup, satisfying 'tests exist and run'.
